column1: # WYSIWYG `:Format Alignment` — Left/Right/Center/General over a range,
# entered via the colon-menu (R3.4a WYSIWYG), POINT-committed.
# Layout: A1 "X", B1 12, C1 "Y".  Each column is 9 chars wide.
# B1 is a number → default right-align ("       12").
# A1/C1 are labels → default left-align (X..., Y...).
# Pointer is parked away from B1 so we read its raw paint, not the
# Default right-align places "12" flush at B1's right edge: zero
# spaces between "12" and the "Y" at C1's left edge.
# `:Format Alignment Left` over B1 — number jumps to the left edge of
# `:Format Alignment Center` over B1 — for "12" in a 9-wide column
# `center_pad` produces 3 leading + 4 trailing spaces, so the row reads
# `:Format Alignment Right` over B1 — back to flush against Y.
# `:Format Alignment General` over B1 — clears the override so the
# cell falls back to the number-default right-align (still flush
